Paths/Backend Development/REST APIs

REST APIs

Design and build RESTful APIs that power web and mobile applications.

REST (Representational State Transfer) is an architectural style for building web APIs.

Key Concepts

  • HTTP Methods: GET (read), POST (create), PUT (update), DELETE (remove).
  • Endpoints: URL patterns like `/api/users` or `/api/posts/:id`.
  • Status Codes: 200 (OK), 201 (Created), 404 (Not Found), 500 (Server Error).
  • JSON: The standard data format for API requests and responses.
  • CRUD: Create, Read, Update, Delete — the four basic operations.

Example

// Next.js API Route
export async function GET(request) {
  const users = await db.users.findMany();
  return Response.json(users);
}

export async function POST(request) {
  const body = await request.json();
  const user = await db.users.create({ data: body });
  return Response.json(user, { status: 201 });
}
